message:
fix(sync): fix Notified poll race when generation changes concurrent with removal

The Notified future's poll implementation had a subtle race: when the
generation changed (indicating notify_waiters was called), the code
would call waiters.remove(index) without first verifying the entry still
existed at that index. If a concurrent notify_one had already consumed
the entry by tail-shrinking the slab, the remove would panic or operate
on the wrong entry.

The fix reorders the logic:
1. First verify the entry exists at the index (index < entries.len())
2. Read the entry's notified flag
3. Only then check generation change and perform removal

The old fallback path for "entry was popped by tail shrinking" is
replaced with an unreachable!() — this state genuinely cannot occur
because we now check existence before any conditional branching.

The stale comment block explaining the unreachable None waker case is
removed since the preceding logic makes it self-evident.

sync/pool.rs: added #[allow(dead_code)] to ObservableGauge fields that
are retained for their Drop-side-effect registration behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
